questions.question: What is the primary goal of backpropagation?
keyPoints: Minimize error between network output and desired output

questions.question: What is the role of the chain rule in backpropagation?
keyPoints: Compute derivative of loss function with respect to model parameters

questions.question: What is the difference between forward pass and backward pass in backpropagation?
keyPoints: Backward pass: compute gradients of loss function with respect to model parameters

questions.question: What is the role of activation functions in backpropagation?
What is the vanishing gradient problem in backpropagation?
keyPoints: Gradients of loss function become very small, making it difficult to update model parameters

questions.question: Explain the concept of backpropagation and its significance in deep learning.
keyPoints: Backpropagation is an algorithm used to train artificial neural networks
Minimizes error between network output and desired output
Uses chain rule to compute derivative of loss function with respect to model parameters

questions.question: Compare and contrast supervised and unsupervised learning in the context of backpropagation.
keyPoints: Supervised learning: training on labeled data
Unsupervised learning: training on unlabeled data
Backpropagation is used in both supervised and unsupervised learning
